lines.speaker: NARRATOR
lines.text: The chapel is the same. Theo is not.

lines.speaker: AGNES
lines.text: You came back.

lines.speaker: THEO
lines.text: Didn't have anywhere else to go.

lines.speaker: AGNES
lines.text: That's honest.

lines.speaker: NARRATOR
lines.text: She stays in her chair by the window. The city beyond is a grid of pale light — indifferent, complete. She has been here long enough that the chapel has stopped feeling like a room and started feeling like a vantage point.

lines.speaker: THEO
lines.text: He won't stop. Whatever Harte is protecting — whoever — one corridor conversation isn't where it ends.

lines.speaker: AGNES
lines.text: No.

lines.speaker: THEO
lines.text: So what do I do with that?

lines.speaker: AGNES
lines.text: What you've already started.

lines.speaker: NARRATOR
lines.text: The heating system ticks somewhere behind the plaster. Theo watches the window rather than her face.

lines.speaker: THEO
lines.text: You said this follows a pattern. What happens to the person who follows it back?

lines.speaker: AGNES
lines.text: The pattern isn't about the person who follows it. It's about the institution. Secrets don't stay buried, Theo. They migrate.

lines.speaker: THEO
lines.text: To where?

lines.speaker: AGNES
lines.text: To whoever the institution can least afford to lose.

lines.speaker: NARRATOR
lines.text: She says it with no particular emphasis, as a fact. He hears what she means.

lines.speaker: THEO
lines.text: You mean me.

lines.speaker: AGNES
lines.text: Because the patients trust you. Because you're good at the job. Because when the inquiry eventually comes — and it will come — having you on record as the one who asked questions makes you the problem. Not the system. You.

lines.speaker: THEO
lines.text: That's how it works.

lines.speaker: AGNES
lines.text: That is how it always works.

lines.speaker: NARRATOR
lines.text: He sits. The light from the corridor finds the edge of his face and stops there.

lines.speaker: THEO
lines.text: I'm not the only one carrying something in this building.

lines.speaker: AGNES
lines.text: No.

lines.speaker: THEO
lines.text: Some of what others are carrying — it's heavier than mine.

lines.speaker: NARRATOR
lines.text: Agnes turns this over. She has been watching this ward for eleven years. She has learned that the weight of a secret is not fixed — it shifts with who's watching and what they stand to lose.

lines.speaker: AGNES
lines.text: Maeve?

lines.speaker: NARRATOR
lines.text: He doesn't answer. He doesn't need to. Agnes has watched Maeve work the last four shifts with the kind of precision that looks like discipline from a distance and looks like something else entirely up close. She has not said anything, because no one has asked her. Dana has not asked. Nobody has asked.

lines.speaker: AGNES
lines.text: There are people in this building who are protected from the outside and exposed from within. That is its own kind of danger. Someone seeing it and saying nothing — that's part of the exposure too.

lines.speaker: THEO
lines.text: Does someone see it?

lines.speaker: AGNES
lines.text: Someone always sees. The question is what they choose to do with it.

lines.speaker: NARRATOR
lines.text: Theo rises. The chapel holds him a moment — the way it holds everything brought into it, briefly, before the shift reclaims it.

lines.speaker: THEO
lines.text: If Harte makes it official — if it comes down to documentation —

lines.speaker: AGNES
lines.text: Then you'll have made the only choice that doesn't require you to lie to yourself at the end of a long night.

lines.speaker: NARRATOR
lines.text: She looks at him with the practiced steadiness of someone who has watched this building metabolize its people for a very long time. Not cruel. Not kind. Simply accurate.

lines.speaker: NARRATOR
lines.text: He goes. The door opens. The shift comes back in — completely, indifferently, without rest. Agnes stays with the window. She counts what she knows. The count has grown.
